original: Hubble Space Telescope sends its first photographs from space
At 7:40 AM,Charles Lindberghtakes off from New York to cross the Atlantic for Paris aboard the Spirit of St. Louis in the first solo nonstop transatlantic flight
US launches Pioneer Venus 1; produces 1st global radar map of Venus
Long-distance train service the Orient Express departs Paris on its last trip to Istanbul (began 1883)
Walter McConnel, 57, is oldest to reach 27,000' Mt Everest top
American rapper Tung Twista raps 597 syllables in under 60 seconds

Alpine village of Blatten, Switzerland evacutes nearly 300 people, 90 sheep, 26 cows (including an injured cow that needed to be ferried out by helicopter), and 20 domestic rabbits, due to concerns of a glacier moving at an alarming pace
Chess grandmasterMagnus Carlsenplays to a draw against an online team of 143,000 players from around the world
Amelia Earhartleaves Newfoundland on her journey to become the 1st woman to fly solo and nonstop across the Atlantic
Aleksey Petrovich Maresyev, Soviet and Russian military pilot known for continuing to fly after losing both legs, born in Kamyshin, Russian Empire (d. 2001)[1][2]
First successful helicopter flight in the US: Vought-Sikorsky VS-300 designed byIgor Sikorskydemonstrated to the public at Bridgeport Airport in Stratford, Connecticut
Toonces The Cat takes the wheel on "Saturday Night Live"
Chicago BullMichael Jordan, named NBA's MVP
Anton Zeilinger, Austrian physicist (2022 Nobel Prize for experiments with entangled photons), born in Innkreis, Austria[1]
Journal Nature publishes findings of the oldest human-made tools from Kenya’s Turkana Basin, dating back 3.3 million years, the first to predate the Homo genus
Israel Kamakawiwo'ole, Native Hawaiian singer, songwriter ("Somewhere Over The Rainbow/What A Wonderful World"), and Hawaiian sovereignty advocate, born in Honolulu, Hawaii (d. 1997)
The Beatles' "Let it Be" documentary film directed by Michael Lindsay-Hogg, premieres at the Liverpool Gaumont Cinema and the London Pavilion; none of the group attend
India launches its 1st satellite independently
10m meteor comes within 150,000 km of Earth (1993KA)
Record set for world's most expensive feather - a huia feather from an extinct New Zealand bird sells for $46,521 NZD (about $28,400 USD) at auction in Auckland
Portuguese explorerVasco da Gamaarrives in Calicut, India, becoming the first European to reach India by sea
Sadaharu Oh, Japanese professional baseball player (Tokyo Yomiuri Giants) and recordholder of most home runs (868), born in Tokyo, Japan[1][2]
Thomas D Akers, American former astronaut (STS 41, 49, 61, 79), born in St. Louis, Missouri

Buster Mathis beats future world heavyweight champion Joe Frazer on points at trials in Flushing, NY to qualify for US Olympic boxing team; Mathis injures thumb, replaced by Frazier who wins gold medal
Battle of Nechtansmere/Dun Nechtain: Picts led by Brude MacBeli beat Northumbrians in Scotland, led by their king Ecgfrith who is killed
Elias Neau forms school for slaves in NY
Citizens of Mecklenburg County, North Carolina declare independence from Great Britain
Saturday Evening Post cover features Norman Rockwell painting
Baseball game in Milwaukee postponed due to dense fog
Larry Holmesbeats Carl Williams in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Bangladesh imposes a 65-day ban on coastal fishing to conserve fish stocks
Cyclone Amphan comes ashore in West Bengal, East India and Bangladesh, with winds of 185 km per hour (115 mph) killing at least 84 people
Common cockroach originated in South Asia, according to new study, then domesticated in Germany before spreading around the world
US PresidentAbraham Lincolnsigns into law the Homestead Act to provide cheap land for the settlement of the American West (80 million acres by 1900)
Levi Straussand Jacob Davis patent the first blue jeans with copper rivets
Decca Records releasesBill Haley& His Comets' hit single "Rock Around the Clock"
Elton Johnis the 1st western pop star to tour USSR
Emile Berliner, German inventor (flat phonograph record, founded Deutsche Grammophon), born in Hanover, Germany (d. 1929)
R. J. [Reginald Joseph] Mitchell, English airplane designer (Supermarine Spitfire), born in Butt Lane, England (d. 1937)
Annie M. G. Schmidt, Dutch writer (Fam Doorsnee, Jip & Janneke), born in Kapelle, Netherlands (d. 1995)
Hal Newhouser, American Baseball HOF pitcher (7 × MLB All-Star; World Series 1945; AL MVP 1944, 45; Triple Crown 1945; Detroit Tigers), born in Detroit, Michigan (d. 1998)
Astrid Kirchherr, German photographer, helped establish The Beatles image, born in Hamburg, Germany (d. 2020)
Boudewijn de Groot, Dutch folk singer ("Sleep Well, Mr. President"), born in a Japanese concentration camp, near Batavia, occupied Dutch East Indies (now Jakarta, Indonesia)
Gotthard rail tunnel between Switzerland & Italy opens
First airplane catapulted from a dirigible, piloted by Charles Nicholson
First regular transatlantic airmail is delivered by Pan Am from New York to Marseille, France
William Congreve, English artillery officer and inventor who designed fire rockets, born in London (d. 1829)
Giuseppe Mercalli, Italian volcanologist and seismologist (Mercalli intensity scale for measuring earthquake intensity), born in Milan, Austrian Empire (d. 1914)[1]
US Navy 1st permitted black recruits to serve
Thor-2A Delta 2 Launch (Norway/USA), Successful
Edward B. Lewis, American geneticist (Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, 1995), born in Wilkes Barre, Pennsylvania (d. 2004)[1]
John Cruickshank, Scottish WWII war hero (Victoria Cross) and banker, born in Aberdeen, Scotland (d. 2025)[1]

Harry "Bud" Grant, American Pro Football HOF coach (NFL Coach of the Year 1969 Minnesota Vikings; 4 x Grey Cup C'ship Winnipeg Blue Bombers) and basketball forward (NBA C'ship 1950 Minnesota Lakers), born in Superior, Wisconsin (d. 2023)[1]
Craig Patrick, American NHL center and executive (USA Olympic gold 1980; GM Pittsburgh Penguins, Stanley Cup 1991-92, 2009), born in Detroit, Michigan
Jane Wiedlin, American pop-rock singer, guitarist, and songwriter (The Go-Gos - "Vacation"; "We Got The Beat"; solo - Fur), born in Oconomowoc, Wisconsin
David Wells, American baseball pitcher (perfect game 1998; World Series 1992, 98; MLB All Star 1995, 98, 2000), born in Torrance, California
Mindy Cohn, American actress (The Facts of Life - "Natalie"), born in Los Angeles, California
Dorchester Massachusetts forms 1st school funded by local taxes
Douglass Hyde receives the first US patent for a fountain pen
York Minster badly damaged by fire
